summary:
Antral follicle count (AFC) closely matches the predictive value of age and FSH for ovarian reserve and response, offering real-time insight into follicular quantity without clearly exceeding their performance in overall IVF success forecasting. It excels at guiding protocol customization during stimulation but doesn't demonstrate universal superiority over other factors such as age-related decline in egg quality or FSH levels indicating reduced reserve.  

(Note: This summary adheres only to clinically significant findings directly related to comparing predictors.)

The provided text contains no information about whether any specific test was used by patients before starting IVF therapy. Therefore, this detail cannot be inferred based solely on available data regarding antral follicle assessments within standard fertility evaluations.

Also note: The retrieved document list includes references to studies involving both women undergoing natural conception attempts and those receiving assisted reproductive technologies including IUIs and IVFs—a point worth noting when interpreting results concerning different patient groups' responses under various interventions.

Finally, one must consider potential limitations inherent in observational cohort designs commonly employed in these types of analyses—for instance, confounding variables affecting reported correlations between biomarkers and live birth outcomes remain unaccounted-for unless explicitly controlled statistically throughout each individual investigation reviewed here.
